Let me first explore the repository to understand what "antidote" is and how it works.

3. How does antidote generate plugin scripts? Find the relevant files.

5. Look at how stdout/stderr are handled during git commands.
6. Find existing tests and how they're structured.

Return a comprehensive summary of your findings including file paths and line numbers for all relevant code.

Now I have a thorough understanding of the repository. Here is my comprehensive summary:

---

## Comprehensive Summary of the Antidote Repository

### 1. What is "Antidote"?

**Language:** Zsh (Z shell scripting). Every function file uses `#!/bin/zsh` shebangs and `emulate -L zsh`.

**Purpose:** Antidote is a feature-complete Zsh plugin manager. It is a successor to/rewrite of [Antibody](https://github.com/getantibody/antibody), which itself was derived from [Antigen](https://github.com/zsh-users/antigen). It provides high-performance, easy-to-use Zsh plugin management with both static (pre-compiled) and dynamic bundle loading modes.

**Current version:** `1.10.2`

**Build system:** A Makefile (`/app/makefile`, 48 lines) with targets for building man pages (`buildman`), running tests (`test`, `unittest`), and version bumping. The core build uses these custom tool scripts:
- `/app/tools/buildman` - Converts `.adoc` man page sources to man pages using `asciidoctor`
- `/app/tools/run-clitests` - Runs tests via the `clitest` framework
- `/app/tools/bumpver` - Bumps version numbers using `bumpversion`

**Key configuration files:**
- `/app/makefile` (48 lines) - Build orchestration
- `/app/.bumpversion.cfg` - Version tracking (currently `1.10.2`)
- `/app/.editorconfig` - Editor formatting rules
- `/app/.github/workflows/test.yml` - CI tests on macOS & Ubuntu with Zsh
- `/app/.github/workflows/test-zsh-5.4.2.yml` - CI tests against Zsh 5.4.2 (minimum supported version)

---

### 2. Overall Directory Structure

```
/app/
├── README.md                    # Project documentation
├── LICENSE                      # MIT License
├── makefile                     # Build/test automation
├── .bumpversion.cfg             # Version tracking
├── .editorconfig                # Editor config
├── .gitignore
├── antidote                     # Top-level executable (function wrapper + dispatch)
├── antidote.zsh                 # Bootstrap loader (source this from .zshrc)
├── functions/                   # Core source code (34 function files)
│   ├── antidote                 # Main entry point wrapper
│   ├── antidote-main            # Main controller/dispatcher
│   ├── antidote-bundle          # Clone bundles & generate static load script
│   ├── antidote-script          # Generate Zsh script for a single bundle
│   ├── antidote-load            # Statically source all bundles
│   ├── antidote-init            # Dynamic mode initialization
│   ├── antidote-update          # Update bundles and self
│   ├── antidote-install         # Install new bundles
│   ├── antidote-purge           # Remove bundles
│   ├── antidote-list            # List cloned bundles
│   ├── antidote-path            # Print path of cloned bundle
│   ├── antidote-home            # Print clone directory
│   ├── antidote-help            # Display man pages or usage
│   ├── __antidote_setup         # Initialize antidote environment
│   ├── __antidote_parser        # Parse bundle DSL to associative array
│   ├── __antidote_parse_bundles # Parse multiple bundles
│   ├── __antidote_bundle_type   # Determine bundle type (repo/url/ssh/file/dir/etc.)
│   ├── __antidote_bundle_dir    # Get clone destination directory
│   ├── __antidote_bundle_name   # Get short name of bundle
│   ├── __antidote_bundle_zcompile # Zsh compile bundles
│   ├── __antidote_bulk_clone    # Generate parallel clone commands
│   ├── __antidote_collect_input # Handle pipe/redirect/argument input
│   ├── __antidote_initfiles     # Find plugin init files in directory
│   ├── __antidote_tourl         # Convert short repo name to URL
│   ├── __antidote_load_prep     # Prepare static file for loading
│   ├── __antidote_version       # Get version string (uses git)
│   ├── __antidote_get_cachedir   # OS-specific cache directory
│   ├── __antidote_print_path    # Pretty-print paths (substitute $HOME)
│   ├── __antidote_indent        # Indent text
│   ├── __antidote_del           # Safe deletion wrapper
│   ├── __antidote_mktemp        # Cross-platform temp file/dir
│   ├── __antidote_supports_color # Check if terminal supports color
│   ├── __antidote_usage         # Print usage text
│   ├── _antidote                # Zsh completion script
│   └── __antidote_load_prep     # Prep to load
├── tests/                       # Test suite (28 markdown test files)
│   ├── README.md                # Test documentation
│   ├── __init__.zsh             # Test bootstrap
│   ├── functions/               # Test helper functions
│   │   ├── t_setup              # Setup test environment
│   │   ├── t_setup_real         # Setup for real (no mocks) tests
│   │   ├── t_teardown           # Teardown test environment
│   │   ├── t_reset              # Reset between test sections
│   │   ├── t_unload_antidote    # Unload antidote functions
│   │   ├── mockgit              # Git mock for testing
│   │   ├── subenv               # Substitute env vars in output
│   │   ├── normalize_aarr       # Normalize assoc array output
│   │   ├── print_aarr           # Print assoc array
│   │   └── aarr_val             # Get value from assoc array
│   ├── fakegitsite.com/         # Fake git repositories for testing
│   ├── testdata/                # Expected test output files
│   ├── tmp_home/                # Template home directory for tests
│   └── *.md                     # Test files (clitest format)
├── tools/                       # Build/CI tools
│   ├── buildman                 # Man page builder
│   ├── run-clitests             # Test runner
│   ├── bumpver                  # Version bumper
│   └── sloc                     # Source lines of code counter
├── man/                         # Man page sources (.adoc) and generated (.1)
├── .github/workflows/           # CI configuration
└── .tmp/                        # Build artifacts (ignored)
```

---

### 3. How Antidote Generates Plugin Scripts

The script generation pipeline flows as follows:

**Entry points:**
- `antidote load` (`/app/functions/antidote-load`, line 24) sources a static file
- `antidote bundle` (`/app/functions/antidote-bundle`, line 9-49) generates the static file

**Core files involved:**

1. **`/app/functions/__antidote_load_prep`** (lines 1-60) - Checks if the static file needs regeneration. At line 51, it calls:
   ```zsh
   antidote bundle <"$bundlefile" >|"$staticfile"
   ```

2. **`/app/functions/antidote-bundle`** (lines 1-49) - The main "bundle" command. It:
   - Collects input via `__antidote_collect_input` (line 23)
   - Optionally generates zcompile wrapper script (lines 27-40)
   - For multiple bundles, does bulk cloning in parallel via `__antidote_bulk_clone` (line 44)
   - Delegates script generation to `__antidote_parse_bundles` (line 48):
     ```zsh
     source <(printf '%s\n' $bundles | __antidote_parse_bundles)
     ```

3. **`/app/functions/__antidote_parse_bundles`** (lines 1-69) - Parses each bundle line (via `__antidote_parser` at line 31), then constructs `antidote-script` invocations with appropriate flags (lines 42-67).

4. **`/app/functions/__antidote_parser`** (lines 1-123) - Converts a bundle DSL string (e.g., `foo/bar kind:defer path:plugins/extract`) into a typed associative array with metadata. Handles:
   - Comments (line 35: `bundle_str=${bundle_str%%\#*}`)
   - Name/kind/path/branch/conditional annotations as key:value pairs
   - Type detection: `repo`, `url`, `sshurl`, `path`, `file`, `dir`, `empty`, `word` (lines 58-74)
   - URL generation for short repos (line 90): `"${gitsite}/${bundle[name]}"` (defaults to `https://github.com/`)
   - Repo directory name sanitization (lines 96-107)

5. **`/app/functions/antidote-script`** (lines 1-216) - The core script generator for a single bundle. It:
   - Resolves the bundle path (line 82)
   - Clones git repos if needed (lines 87-91):
     ```zsh
     git clone --quiet --recurse-submodules --shallow-submodules $o_branch $giturl $bundle_path
     ```
   - Generates `fpath`, `source`, `autoload`, `PATH export` commands based on the `kind` (fpath/path/zsh/defer/clone/autoload at lines 112-215)
   - Handles `pre`/`post` load hooks (lines 114, 197-203)
   - Handles `conditional` wrappers (lines 209-213)
   - Handles defer with `zsh-defer` integration (lines 121-130)

6. **`/app/functions/__antidote_bulk_clone`** (lines 1-38) - Uses `awk` to parse bundle lines and generate background `antidote-script --kind clone` commands with `&` for parallel cloning, followed by `wait` (line 36).

7. **`/app/functions/antidote-init`** (lines 1-33) - For dynamic mode, generates a Zsh script that overrides the `antidote` function to source `antidote bundle` output directly instead of writing to a static file.

---

### 4. How Antidote Uses Git

Git is invoked in the following locations:

| File | Line(s) | Git Command | Purpose |
|------|---------|-------------|---------|
| `/app/functions/antidote-script` | 90 | `git clone --quiet --recurse-submodules --shallow-submodules $o_branch $giturl $bundle_path` | Clone a plugin repo |
| `/app/functions/antidote-update` | 65 | `git -C "$bundledir" config remote.origin.url` | Get remote URL of a bundle |
| `/app/functions/antidote-update` | 73 | `git -C "$1" rev-parse --short HEAD` | Get current SHA (pre-update) |
| `/app/functions/antidote-update` | 81 | `git -C "$1" rev-parse --is-shallow-repository 2>/dev/null` | Check if shallow clone |
| `/app/functions/antidote-update` | 82 | `git -C "$1" fetch --quiet --unshallow` | Unshallow a shallow clone |
| `/app/functions/antidote-update` | 84 | `git -C "$1" fetch --quiet` | Fetch updates |
| `/app/functions/antidote-update` | 87 | `git -C "$1" pull --quiet --ff --rebase --autostash` | Pull updates |
| `/app/functions/antidote-update` | 88 | `git -C "$1" submodule --quiet sync --recursive` | Sync submodules |
| `/app/functions/antidote-update` | 89 | `git -C "$1" submodule --quiet update --init --recursive --depth 1` | Update submodules |
| `/app/functions/antidote-update` | 90 | `git -C "$1" rev-parse --short HEAD` | Get new SHA (post-update) |
| `/app/functions/antidote-update` | 96 | `git -C "$1" --no-pager log --oneline --ancestry-path --first-parent "${oldsha}^..${newsha}" 2>/dev/null` | Show changelog between old and new SHA |
| `/app/functions/antidote-update` | 147 | `git -C "${0:A:h:h}" pull --quiet --ff --rebase --autostash` | Self-update antidote |
| `/app/functions/antidote-list` | 51 | `git -C "$bundledir" config remote.origin.url` | Get URL of each installed bundle |
| `/app/functions/__antidote_version` | 8 | `git -C "${0:A:h:h}" rev-parse --short HEAD 2>/dev/null` | Get SHA for version display |

**All git commands use `--quiet`** (`/app/functions/antidote-update` lines 82, 84, 87, 88, 89, 147; `/app/functions/antidote-script` line 90), suppressing normal git progress output.

---

### 5. How stdout/stderr Are Handled During Git Commands

**In `antidote-script` (cloning):**
- Line 89: `print -ru2 "# antidote cloning $bundle_name..."` - Prints status message to **stderr**
- Line 90: `git clone --quiet ...` - Output goes to default stdout/stderr (suppressed by `--quiet`)
- Line 91: `[[ $? -eq 0 ]] || return 1` - Only checks exit code; **no redirection** of git output

**In `antidote-update` (background parallel updates):**
- Lines 103: Everything inside the subshell `()` (lines 70-104) is captured:
  ```zsh
  } > "$tmpfile" 2>&1
  ```
  All stdout and stderr from git commands go to a temp file, then printed sequentially later (lines 112-135).
- Lines 76-77: Isolates git from user config:
  ```zsh
  local GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL=/dev/null
  local GIT_CONFIG_SYSTEM=/dev/null
  ```
- Line 81: `2>/dev/null` suppresses stderr from `rev-parse --is-shallow-repository`
- Line 96: `2>/dev/null` suppresses stderr from the log command
- Line 147: Self-update `git pull --quiet` goes to stdout/stderr directly (not redirected)

**In `__antidote_version`:**
- Line 8: `2>/dev/null` suppresses stderr from `rev-parse --short HEAD` (in case antidote itself is not git-managed)

**In `antidote-list`:**
- Line 51: `git -C "$bundledir" config remote.origin.url` - No redirection; output captured via `$()` command substitution

**In tests (`mockgit`):**
- `/app/tests/functions/mockgit` lines 1-73: A mock function that handles git commands without actually running git. It writes to stdout silently when `--quiet` flag is present (line 60: checks `$#o_quiet`), otherwise prints mock messages. Unrecognized commands print to stderr (line 70: `echo >&2 "mocking not implemented..."`)

---

### 6. How Tests Are Structured

**Test framework:** [clitest](https://github.com/aureliojargas/clitest) - a "literate test" framework that uses markdown files. Tests are run via `/app/tools/run-clitests` (lines 1-38).

**Test file format:**
- Markdown files (`.md`) in `/app/tests/` with embedded Zsh code blocks
- Lines starting with `%` are commands; subsequent lines are expected output
- Special syntax: `#=> --exit N` for exit code assertions, `#=> --file <path>` for output comparison
- Prompts use `%` character

**Test lifecycle (per markdown file):**
1. **Setup:** Source `__init__.zsh` then call `t_setup` (or `t_setup_real`)
2. **Tests:** Commands with expected output/exit codes
3. **Teardown:** Call `t_teardown`

**`t_setup`** (`/app/tests/functions/t_setup`, lines 1-65):
- Creates a temporary `$T_TEMPDIR` using `mktemp`
- Sets `$HOME`, `$ZDOTDIR`, `$ANTIDOTE_HOME` to temp paths
- Copies `tests/tmp_home/` as the template home
- Copies testdata into temp dir
- Replaces `.mockgit` directories with `.git`
- **Mocks git** with `function git { mockgit "$@" }` (line 24)
- Sources `antidote.zsh`

**`t_setup_real`** (`/app/tests/functions/t_setup_real`, lines 1-34):
- Extends `t_setup` but then unmocks git, allowing actual cloning from real repos
- Used only in `test_real.md`

**`t_teardown`** (`/app/tests/functions/t_teardown`, lines 1-47):
- Restores `$HOME`, `$ZDOTDIR`
- Unfunctions all antidote functions and the git mock
- Restores `$path`, `$fpath`, zstyles
- Removes temp directory

**Test files (28 total):**

| File | What It Tests |
|------|---------------|
| `test_antidote.md` | Version display |
| `test_antidote_core.md` | Core version |
| `test_cmd_bundle.md` | `antidote bundle` command |
| `test_cmd_script.md` | `antidote-script` - all script generation options |
| `test_cmd_load.md` | `antidote load` command |
| `test_cmd_init.md` | `antidote init` dynamic mode |
| `test_cmd_update.md` | `antidote update` |
| `test_cmd_install.md` | `antidote install` |
| `test_cmd_purge.md` | `antidote purge` |
| `test_cmd_list.md` | `antidote list` |
| `test_cmd_home.md` | `antidote home` |
| `test_cmd_path.md` | `antidote path` |
| `test_cmd_help.md` | `antidote help` |
| `test_cmd_main.md` | Main controller and lazy loading |
| `test_helpers.md` | Private helper functions |
| `test_parser.md` | Bundle parser |
| `test_bundle_helpers.md` | Bundle helper functions |
| `test_mockgit.md` | Git mock functionality |
| `test_fpath_rules.md` | fpath rules (append/prepend) |
| `test_paths_with_spaces.md` | Paths containing spaces |
| `test_supports_color.md` | Color support detection |
| `test_zcompile_bundle.md` | Zsh compilation |
| `test_zcompile_static.md` | Static file compilation |
| `test_real.md` | Integration tests with real git repos |
| `test_alt_zsh_defer_bundle.md` | Alternative defer bundle paths |
| `test_setopts_special.md` | Special shell options |
| `test_zsetopts.md` | Zsh options handling |
| `README.md` | Self-tests the framework itself |

**Mock git** (`/app/tests/functions/mockgit`, lines 1-73):
Handles: `--version`, `clone`, `config remote.origin.url`, `pull`, `rev-parse HEAD`, `submodule`, `fetch`, and `--no-pager log`. Clones from `/app/tests/fakegitsite.com/` directory. Unrecognized commands print to stderr and return 1.

---

### 7. Key Configuration Files

- **`/app/makefile`** (48 lines) - Default target `help`. Main targets: `build`, `buildman`, `test`, `unittest`, `bump-maj`, `bump-min`, `bump-rev`.

- **`/app/.bumpversion.cfg`** (16 lines) - Version `1.10.2`, updates in: `functions/__antidote_version`, `tests/README.md`, `tests/test_antidote.md`, `tests/test_antidote_core.md`, `tests/test_cmd_update.md`, `README.md`.

- **`/app/.editorconfig`** (19 lines) - UTF-8, LF line endings, 2-space indentation (tabs for makefile only), no trailing whitespace in most files (except markdown).

- **`/app/.gitignore`** (14 lines) - Ignores zwc (compiled Zsh), `.tmp/`, `.plugins/`, temp and backup files.

- **`/app/.github/workflows/test.yml`** (23 lines) - GitHub Actions CI on push/PR: macOS + Ubuntu, uses `clitest`, runs `make test` with Zsh.

- **`/app/.github/workflows/test-zsh-5.4.2.yml`** (33 lines) - Builds Zsh 5.4.2 from source, verifies minimum version compatibility.

- **`/app/antidote.zsh`** (17 lines) - Bootstrap file: checks for Zsh >= 5.4.2, sources `__antidote_setup` to initialize.

- **`/app/antidote`** (17 lines) - Top-level executable script: defines `antidote` function, lazy-loads `antidote.zsh` if needed, dispatches to `antidote-main "$@"`.
</task_result>
</task>

part.text:
Now I have a thorough understanding of the repository. Let me analyze the problem:

**The Bug:** A git wrapper on PATH writes progress to stdout even for `git --quiet`. Antidote is leaking that text into generated plugin scripts.

The key issue is in `antidote-script` (and possibly other functions) where git commands are invoked with `--quiet` but their stdout is not redirected. If a git wrapper ignores `--quiet` and writes progress to stdout, that text gets captured and ends up in the generated script output.

Let me look at the critical locations:

1. `functions/antidote-script` line 90: `git clone --quiet --recurse-submodules ...` - This runs during `antidote bundle` / `antidote script` and its stdout could leak into the generated script.

2. `functions/__antidote_version` line 8: `git rev-parse --short HEAD 2>/dev/null` - This uses command substitution, so stdout leak would affect the version string.

3. `functions/antidote-list` line 51: `git -C "$bundledir" config remote.origin.url` - Also command substitution.

4. `functions/antidote-update` - Most git commands here are wrapped in a subshell with stdout/stderr redirected to a temp file (line 103: `} > "$tmpfile" 2>&1`), so those are fine.

The main concern is `antidote-script` where `git clone --quiet` runs and its output could be emitted to stdout which IS the generated plugin script. Let me read the actual files to verify.

#!/bin/zsh

### Generate the Zsh script to load a plugin.
#
# usage: antidote script [-h|--help] [-k|--kind <kind>] [-p|--path <path>]
#                        [-c|--conditional <func>] [-b|--branch <branch>]
#                        [--pre <func>] [--post <func>] [--skip-load-defer]
#                        [-a|--autoload <path>] <bundle>
# <kind>   : zsh,path,fpath,defer,clone,autoload
# <path>   : Relative path from the bundle root
# <branch> : The git branch
# <bundle> : A bundle can be a directory, a zsh script, or a git repo
#

### Generate the Zsh script to load a plugin.
#function antidote-script {
  emulate -L zsh; setopt local_options $_adote_funcopts
  local MATCH MBEGIN MEND; local -a match mbegin mend  # appease 'warn_create_global'
  local REPLY=

  local -a o_help o_kind o_path o_branch o_cond o_autoload o_pre o_post o_fpath_rule o_skip_load_defer
  zparseopts $_adote_zparopt_flags -- \
    h=o_help       -help=h            \
    a:=o_autoload  -autoload:=a       \
    b:=o_branch    -branch:=b         \
    k:=o_kind      -kind:=k           \
    p:=o_path      -path:=p           \
                   -pre:=o_pre        \
                   -post:=o_post      \
                   -fpath-rule:=o_fpath_rule \
                   -skip-load-defer=o_skip_load_defer \
    c:=o_cond      -conditional:=c    ||
    return 1

  # set defaults
  (( $#o_kind )) || o_kind=(--kind zsh)
  if ! (( $#o_fpath_rule )); then
    zstyle -a ':antidote:fpath' rule 'o_fpath_rule' || o_fpath_rule=(append)
  fi

  # strip '=' or ':' from beginning of arg values
  local re='^[=:]?(.+)$'
  [[ $o_kind[-1] =~ $re ]] && o_kind[-1]=$match
  [[ $o_autoload[-1] =~ $re ]] && o_autoload[-1]=$match
  [[ $o_path[-1] =~ $re ]] && o_path[-1]=$match
  [[ $o_cond[-1] =~ $re ]] && o_cond[-1]=$match
  [[ $o_branch[-1] =~ $re ]] && o_branch[-1]=$match
  [[ $o_pre[-1] =~ $re ]] && o_pre[-1]=$match
  [[ $o_post[-1] =~ $re ]] && o_post[-1]=$match
  [[ $o_fpath_rule[-1] =~ $re ]] && o_fpath_rule[-1]=$match

  local supported_kind_vals=(autoload clone defer fpath path zsh)
  if (( $#o_kind )) && ! (( $supported_kind_vals[(Ie)$o_kind[-1]] )); then
    print -ru2 "antidote: error: unexpected kind value: '$o_kind[-1]'" && return 1
  fi

  # If no fpath_rule is set, use the zstyle
  if ! (( $#o_fpath_rule )); then
    local zstyle_fpath_rule
    zstyle -s ':antidote:fpath' rule 'zstyle_fpath_rule'
    [[ -z "$zstyle_fpath_rule" ]] || o_fpath_rule=($zstyle_fpath_rule)
  fi

  local supported_fpath_rules=(append prepend)
  if ! (( $supported_fpath_rules[(Ie)$o_fpath_rule[-1]] )); then
    print -ru2 "antidote: error: unexpected fpath rule: '$o_fpath_rule[-1]'" && return 1
  fi

  local bundle=$1
  if [[ -z "$bundle" ]]; then
    print -ru2 "antidote: error: bundle argument expected" && return 1
  fi
  local bundle_name=$(__antidote_bundle_name $bundle)

  # replace ~/ with $HOME/
  if [[ "$bundle" == '~/'* ]]; then
    bundle=$HOME/${bundle#'~/'*}
  fi

  # set the path to the bundle (repo or local)
  local bundle_path
  [[ -e "$bundle" ]] && bundle_path=$bundle || bundle_path=$(__antidote_bundle_dir $bundle)

  # handle cloning repo bundles
  local bundle_type
  bundle_type="$(__antidote_bundle_type $bundle)"
  if [[ "$bundle_type" == (repo|url|sshurl) ]] && [[ ! -e "$bundle_path" ]]; then
    local giturl=$(__antidote_tourl $bundle)
    print -ru2 "# antidote cloning $bundle_name..."
    git clone --quiet --recurse-submodules --shallow-submodules $o_branch $giturl $bundle_path
    [[ $? -eq 0 ]] || return 1
  fi

  # if we only needed to clone the bundle, compile and we're done
  if [[ "$o_kind[-1]" == "clone" ]]; then
    if zstyle -t ":antidote:bundle:$bundle" zcompile; then
      __antidote_bundle_zcompile $bundle_path
    fi
    return
  fi

  # add path to bundle
  [[ -n "$o_path[-1]" ]] && bundle_path+="/$o_path[-1]"

  # handle defer pre-reqs first
  local dopts zsh_defer='zsh-defer'
  zstyle -s ":antidote:bundle:${bundle}" defer-options 'dopts'
  [[ -n "$dopts" ]] && zsh_defer="zsh-defer $dopts"

  # generate the script
  local -a script=()

  # add pre-load function
  (( $#o_pre )) && script+=("$o_pre[-1]")

  # handle defers
  local source_cmd="source"
  local zsh_defer_bundle
  zstyle -s ':antidote:defer' bundle 'zsh_defer_bundle' \
    || zsh_defer_bundle='romkatv/zsh-defer'
  if [[ "$o_kind[-1]" == "defer" ]]; then
    source_cmd="${zsh_defer} source"
    if ! (( $#o_skip_load_defer )); then
      script+=(
        'if ! (( $+functions[zsh-defer] )); then'
        "$(antidote-script $zsh_defer_bundle | __antidote_indent)"
        'fi'
      )
    fi
  fi

  # Let's make the path a little nicer to deal with
  local print_bundle_path="$(__antidote_print_path "$bundle_path")"

  # handle autoloading before sourcing
  if (( $#o_autoload )); then
    if [[ "$o_fpath_rule[-1]" == prepend ]]; then
      script+=("fpath=( \"${print_bundle_path}/${o_autoload[-1]}\" \$fpath )")
      script+=("builtin autoload -Uz \$fpath[1]/*(N.:t)")
    else
      script+=("fpath+=( \"${print_bundle_path}/${o_autoload[-1]}\" )")
      script+=("builtin autoload -Uz \$fpath[-1]/*(N.:t)")
    fi
  fi

  # generate load script
  bundle_type="$(__antidote_bundle_type $bundle_path)"
  local fpath_script
  if [[ "$o_fpath_rule[-1]" == prepend ]]; then
    fpath_script="fpath=( \"$print_bundle_path\" \$fpath )"
  else
    fpath_script="fpath+=( \"$print_bundle_path\" )"
  fi

  if [[ "$o_kind[-1]" == fpath ]]; then
    # fpath
    script+="$fpath_script"
  elif [[ "$o_kind[-1]" == path ]]; then
    # path
    script+="export PATH=\"$print_bundle_path:\$PATH\""
  elif [[ "$o_kind[-1]" == autoload ]]; then
    # autoload
    script+=("$fpath_script")
    if [[ "$o_fpath_rule[-1]" == prepend ]]; then
      script+=("builtin autoload -Uz \$fpath[1]/*(N.:t)")
    else
      script+=("builtin autoload -Uz \$fpath[-1]/*(N.:t)")
    fi
  else
    if zstyle -t ":antidote:bundle:$bundle" zcompile; then
      __antidote_bundle_zcompile $bundle_path
    fi
    if [[ $bundle_type == file ]]; then
      script+="$source_cmd \"$print_bundle_path\""
    else
      # directory/default
      local initfile initfiles
      initfiles=(${(@f)$(__antidote_initfiles $bundle_path)})
      # if no init file was found, assume the default
      if [[ $#initfiles -eq 0 ]]; then
        if (( $#o_path )); then
          initfiles=($bundle_path/${bundle_path:t}.plugin.zsh)
        else
          initfiles=($bundle_path/${bundle_name:t}.plugin.zsh)
        fi
      fi
      script+="$fpath_script"
      local print_initfile
      for initfile in $initfiles; do
        print_initfile="$(__antidote_print_path "$initfile")"
        script+="$source_cmd \"$print_initfile\""
      done
    fi
  fi

  # add post-load function
  if (( $#o_post )); then
    if [[ "$o_kind[-1]" == "defer" ]]; then
      script+=("${zsh_defer} $o_post[-1]")
    else
      script+=("$o_post[-1]")
    fi
  fi

  # mark bundle as loaded
  # script+="zstyle ':antidote:bundle:${bundle_name}' loaded yes"

  # wrap conditional
  if [[ -n "$o_cond[-1]" ]]; then
    print "if $o_cond[-1]; then"
    printf "  %s\n" $script
    print "fi"
  else
    printf "%s\n" $script
  fi
#}

#!/bin/zsh

### Update antidote and its cloned bundles.
#
# usage: antidote update [-h|--help]
#
#function antidote-update {
  0=${(%):-%x}
  emulate -L zsh; setopt local_options $_adote_funcopts

  local o_help o_self o_bundles
  zparseopts $_adote_zparopt_flags -- \
    h=o_help    -help=h    \
    s=o_self    -self=s    \
    b=o_bundles -bundles=b ||
    return 1

  if (( $#o_help )); then
    antidote-help update
    return
  fi

  # colors
  local green blue normal
  if __antidote_supports_color; then
    if (( $+commands[tput] )); then
      green=$(tput setaf 2)
      blue=$(tput setaf 4)
      normal=$(tput sgr0)
    else
      green=$'\E[32m'
      blue=$'\E[34m'
      normal=$'\E[0m'
    fi
  fi

  if (( $#o_bundles )) || ! (( $#o_self )); then
    print "Updating bundles..."
    local bundledir url repo

    # remove zcompiled files
    __antidote_del -rf -- $(antidote-home)/**/*.zwc(N)

    # remove check file
    local loadable_check_path="$(antidote-home)/.antidote.load"
    [[ -r "$loadable_check_path" ]] && __antidote_del -- "$loadable_check_path"

    # Setup temporary directory and tracking
    local tmpfile
    typeset -g __antidote_update_tmpdir=$(__antidote_mktemp -d -s update)
    local tmpdir=$__antidote_update_tmpdir

    # Cleanup function to ensure we don't leave temp files behind
    __antidote_update_cleanup() {
      [[ -d "$__antidote_update_tmpdir" ]] && __antidote_del -rf -- "$__antidote_update_tmpdir"
      unset __antidote_update_tmpdir
    }

    # Set trap to ensure cleanup on exit, interrupt, etc.
    # (EXIT is special, 2=INT, 15=TERM, 1=HUP)
    trap __antidote_update_cleanup EXIT 2 15 1

    # update all bundles
    for bundledir in $(antidote-list --dirs); do
      url=$(git -C "$bundledir" config remote.origin.url)
      repo="${url:h:t}/${${url:t}%.git}"
      print "antidote: checking for updates: $url"

      () {
        # Create a temporary output file in our temp directory
        local repo_id="${repo//\//-SLASH-}"
        local tmpfile="${tmpdir}/${repo_id}.output"
        local oldsha=$(git -C "$1" rev-parse --short HEAD)

        # Set environment variables to isolate git from user config
        local GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL=/dev/null
        local GIT_CONFIG_SYSTEM=/dev/null

        # Unshallow the repo, because with the SHA locking feature coming in v2, we'll
        # need to have everything.
        if git -C "$1" rev-parse --is-shallow-repository 2>/dev/null | grep -q "true" || [[ -f "$1/.git/shallow" ]]; then
          git -C "$1" fetch --quiet --unshallow
        else
          git -C "$1" fetch --quiet
        fi

        git -C "$1" pull --quiet --ff --rebase --autostash
        git -C "$1" submodule --quiet sync --recursive
        git -C "$1" submodule --quiet update --init --recursive --depth 1
        local newsha=$(git -C "$1" rev-parse --short HEAD)

        # Capture all output to temporary file
        {
          if [[ $oldsha != $newsha ]]; then
            print -- "${green}antidote: updated: $2 ${oldsha} -> ${newsha}${normal}"
            git -C "$1" --no-pager log --oneline --ancestry-path --first-parent "${oldsha}^..${newsha}" 2>/dev/null
          fi

          # recompile bundles
          if zstyle -t ":antidote:bundle:$repo" zcompile; then
            __antidote_bundle_zcompile $bundledir
          fi
        } > "$tmpfile" 2>&1
      } "$bundledir" "$url" &
    done

    print "Waiting for bundle updates to complete..."
    print ""
    wait

    # Display all output in sequence
    for tmpfile in "$tmpdir"/*.output(N); do
      if [[ -s "$tmpfile" ]]; then
        # Extract the repo id from the filename and decode it
        local filename=${tmpfile:t}
        local repo_id=${filename%.output}
        repo_id=${repo_id//-SLASH-/\/}

        print "${blue}Bundle ${repo_id} update check complete.${normal}"

        # Colorize the SHA
        ${__adote_awkcmd:-awk} '
          BEGIN {
              YELLOW="\033[33m"
              NORMAL="\033[0m"
          }
          NF >= 1 {
            printf "%s%s%s %s\n", YELLOW, $1, NORMAL, substr($0, length($1) + 2)
            next
          }
          { print }
          END { print "" }
        ' "$tmpfile"
      fi
    done

    # The trap will do this cleanup too, but let's run this ASAP.
    __antidote_update_cleanup
    print "${green}Bundle updates complete.${normal}"
    print ""
  fi

  # update antidote
  if (( $#o_self )) || ! (( $#o_bundles )); then
    print "Updating antidote..."
    if [[ -d "${0:A:h:h}/.git" ]]; then
      git -C "${0:A:h:h}" pull --quiet --ff --rebase --autostash
      print "antidote self-update complete.\n"

      # setup antidote again
      (( $+functions[__antidote_setup] )) && unfunction __antidote_setup
      builtin autoload -Uz ${0:A:h}/__antidote_setup
      __antidote_setup

      # show antidote version
      antidote -v
    else
      print "Self updating is disabled in this build."
      print "Use your OS package manager to update antidote itself."
    fi
  fi
#}

2. **`antidote-update:65`** - `url=$(git -C "$bundledir" config remote.origin.url)`

3. **`antidote-update:73`** - `local oldsha=$(git -C "$1" rev-parse --short HEAD)`

5. **`antidote-update:82-89`** - All git commands with `--quiet`, but they're inside a subshell `{ ... } > "$tmpfile" 2>&1` at line 103. So stdout/stderr goes to temp file. Safe.

The fix: redirect `git clone` stdout to /dev/null. This way, even if a wrapper ignores `--quiet` and writes to stdout, those messages won't leak. Error messages on stderr (like actual git errors) will still be visible to the user.

For the command substitution cases (`antidote-update:65,73,90`, `antidote-list:51`, `__antidote_version:8`), stdout is captured by `$()`, so any wrapper progress messages would pollute the captured value. #!/bin/zsh
#function mockgit {
  # handle these commands:
  # - `git -C "$dir" config remote.origin.url`
  # - `git -C "$dir" pull --quiet --ff --rebase --autostash`
  # - `git -C "$dir" rev-parse --short HEAD`
  # - `git clone --quiet --depth 1 --recurse-submodules --shallow-submodules --branch branch $url $dir`
  # - `git --version`
  emulate -L zsh; setopt local_options extended_glob
  local MATCH MBEGIN MEND; local -a match mbegin mend  # appease 'warn_create_global'
  0=${(%):-%x}
  local prjdir="${0:A:h:h:h}"
  local args=("$@[@]")
  local o_path o_quiet o_ff o_rebase o_autostash o_short
  local o_depth o_recurse_submodules o_shallow_submodules o_branch
  local o_init o_recursive
  zparseopts -D -E --      \
    C:=o_path              \
    -short=o_short         \
    -quiet=o_quiet         \
    -ff=o_ff               \
    -rebase=o_rebase       \
    -autostash=o_autostash \
    -recurse-submodules=o_recurse_submodules \
    -shallow-submodules=o_shallow_submodules \
    -depth:=o_depth                          \
    -branch:=o_branch                        \
    -init=o_init                             \
    -recursive=o_recursive                  ||
    return 1

  if [[ "$@" = "--version" ]]; then
    echo "mockgit version 0.0.0"
  elif [[ "$1" = "clone" ]]; then
    local giturl="$2"
    local repo="${giturl:h:t}/${${giturl:t}%.git}"
    local bundledir="${3:-.}"
    local gitsite_repodir="$prjdir/tests/fakegitsite.com/${repo}"
    if [[ -d "$gitsite_repodir" ]]; then
      [[ -d "${bundledir:A}" ]] || mkdir -p "${bundledir:A}"
      cp -r -- "$gitsite_repodir" "${bundledir:A}"
    elif ! (( $#o_quiet )); then
      echo "MOCKGIT: Cloning into '${repo:t}'..."
      echo "MOCKGIT: Repository not found."
      echo "MOCKGIT: repository '$giturl' not found"
    fi
  elif [[ "$@" = "config remote.origin.url" ]]; then
    if [[ -e $bundledir/.git/config/remote.origin.url ]]; then
      cat $bundledir/.git/config/remote.origin.url
    else
      # un-sanitize dir into URL
      local url=$o_path[-1]
      url=${url:t}
      url=${url:gs/-AT-/\@}
      url=${url:gs/-COLON-/\:}
      url=${url:gs/-SLASH-/\/}
      echo "$url"
    fi
  elif [[ "$@" = "pull" ]]; then
    (( $#o_quiet )) || echo "MOCKGIT: Already up to date."
  elif [[ "$@" = "rev-parse HEAD" ]]; then
    if (( $#o_short )); then
      echo "abcd123"
    else
      echo "abcd1230abcd1230abcd1230abcd1230abcd1230"
    fi
  elif [[ "$1" == (submodule|fetch) ]]; then
    # nothing to do
  else
    echo >&2 "mocking not implemented for git command: git $@"
    return 1
  fi
#}

# antidote-script tests

## Setup

```zsh
% source ./tests/__init__.zsh
% t_setup
%
```

## Script Command

### Fails

```zsh
% antidote-script  #=> --exit 1
% antidote-script
antidote: error: bundle argument expected
%
```

### Arg style

`antidote-script` accepts '--arg val', '--arg:val', '--arg=val' syntax

```zsh
% antidote-script --kind zsh foo/bar  #=> --exit 0
% antidote-script --kind:zsh foo/bar  #=> --exit 0
% antidote-script --kind=zsh foo/bar  #=> --exit 0
% antidote-script --kind+zsh foo/bar  #=> --exit 1
%
```

### Scripting types

`antidote-script` works with local files and directories, as well as remote repos.

Script a file:

```zsh
% antidote-script $ZDOTDIR/aliases.zsh | subenv ZDOTDIR
source "$ZDOTDIR/aliases.zsh"
%
```

Script a lib directory:

```zsh
% antidote-script $ZDOTDIR/custom/lib | subenv ZDOTDIR
fpath+=( "$ZDOTDIR/custom/lib" )
source "$ZDOTDIR/custom/lib/lib1.zsh"
source "$ZDOTDIR/custom/lib/lib2.zsh"
%
```

Script a plugin directory:

```zsh
% antidote-script $ZDOTDIR/custom/plugins/myplugin | subenv ZDOTDIR
fpath+=( "$ZDOTDIR/custom/plugins/myplugin" )
source "$ZDOTDIR/custom/plugins/myplugin/myplugin.plugin.zsh"
%
```

Script repos in antibody style:

```zsh
% zstyle ':antidote:bundle' use-friendly-names off
% ANTIDOTE_HOME=$HOME/.cache/antibody
% antidote-script foo/bar                        | subenv ANTIDOTE_HOME  #=> --file ./testdata/antibody/script-foobar.zsh
% antidote-script https://github.com/foo/bar     | subenv ANTIDOTE_HOME  #=> --file ./testdata/antibody/script-foobar.zsh
% antidote-script https://github.com/foo/bar.git | subenv ANTIDOTE_HOME  #=> --file ./testdata/antibody/script-foobar.zsh
% antidote-script git@github.com:foo/qux.git     | subenv ANTIDOTE_HOME  #=> --file ./testdata/antibody/script-fooqux.zsh
% zstyle ':antidote:bundle' use-friendly-names on
% ANTIDOTE_HOME=$HOME/.cache/antidote
%
```

## Annotations

### kind:clone

Nothing happens when the plugin already exists.

```zsh
% antidote-script --kind clone foo/bar
%
```

Clone a missing plugin.

```zsh
% antidote-script --kind clone bar/foo
# antidote cloning bar/foo...
%
```

### kind:zsh

```zsh
% antidote-script --kind zsh foo/bar | subenv ANTIDOTE_HOME
fpath+=( "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/foo/bar" )
source "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/foo/bar/bar.plugin.zsh"
%
```

### kind:path

```zsh
% antidote-script --kind path foo/bar | subenv ANTIDOTE_HOME
export PATH="$ANTIDOTE_HOME/foo/bar:$PATH"
%
```

### kind:fpath

```zsh
% antidote-script --kind fpath foo/bar | subenv ANTIDOTE_HOME
fpath+=( "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/foo/bar" )
%
```

### kind:autoload

```zsh
% antidote-script --kind autoload $ZDOTDIR/functions | subenv ZDOTDIR
fpath+=( "$ZDOTDIR/functions" )
builtin autoload -Uz $fpath[-1]/*(N.:t)
%
```

### kind:defer

```zsh
% antidote-script --kind defer foo/bar | subenv ANTIDOTE_HOME
if ! (( $+functions[zsh-defer] )); then
  fpath+=( "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/getantidote/zsh-defer" )
  source "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/getantidote/zsh-defer/zsh-defer.plugin.zsh"
fi
fpath+=( "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/foo/bar" )
zsh-defer source "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/foo/bar/bar.plugin.zsh"
%
```

Test skipping defer loading

```zsh
% antidote-script --kind defer --skip-load-defer foo/bar | subenv ANTIDOTE_HOME
fpath+=( "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/foo/bar" )
zsh-defer source "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/foo/bar/bar.plugin.zsh"
%
```

Test defer zstyle settings

```zsh
% zstyle ':antidote:bundle:*' defer-options '-a'
% zstyle ':antidote:bundle:foo/bar' defer-options '-p'
% antidote-script --kind defer foo/bar | subenv ANTIDOTE_HOME
if ! (( $+functions[zsh-defer] )); then
  fpath+=( "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/getantidote/zsh-defer" )
  source "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/getantidote/zsh-defer/zsh-defer.plugin.zsh"
fi
fpath+=( "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/foo/bar" )
zsh-defer -p source "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/foo/bar/bar.plugin.zsh"
%
% # Uses different defer options due to zstyle matching
% antidote-script --kind defer bar/baz 2>/dev/null | subenv ANTIDOTE_HOME
if ! (( $+functions[zsh-defer] )); then
  fpath+=( "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/getantidote/zsh-defer" )
  source "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/getantidote/zsh-defer/zsh-defer.plugin.zsh"
fi
fpath+=( "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/bar/baz" )
zsh-defer -a source "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/bar/baz/baz.plugin.zsh"
% # cleanup
% t_reset
%
```

### path:plugin-dir

```zsh
% antidote-script --path plugins/extract ohmy/ohmy | subenv ANTIDOTE_HOME
fpath+=( "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/ohmy/ohmy/plugins/extract" )
source "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/ohmy/ohmy/plugins/extract/extract.plugin.zsh"
%
```

### path:file

```zsh
% antidote-script --path lib/lib1.zsh ohmy/ohmy | subenv ANTIDOTE_HOME
source "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/ohmy/ohmy/lib/lib1.zsh"
%
```

### path:lib-dir

```zsh
% antidote-script --path lib ohmy/ohmy | subenv ANTIDOTE_HOME
fpath+=( "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/ohmy/ohmy/lib" )
source "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/ohmy/ohmy/lib/lib1.zsh"
source "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/ohmy/ohmy/lib/lib2.zsh"
source "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/ohmy/ohmy/lib/lib3.zsh"
%
```

### path:theme

```zsh
% antidote-script --path themes/pretty.zsh-theme ohmy/ohmy | subenv ANTIDOTE_HOME
source "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/ohmy/ohmy/themes/pretty.zsh-theme"
%
```

### conditional:testfunc

```zsh
% antidote-script --conditional is-macos --path plugins/macos ohmy/ohmy | subenv ANTIDOTE_HOME
if is-macos; then
  fpath+=( "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/ohmy/ohmy/plugins/macos" )
  source "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/ohmy/ohmy/plugins/macos/macos.plugin.zsh"
fi
%
```

### autoload:funcdir

```zsh
% antidote-script --path plugins/macos --autoload functions ohmy/ohmy | subenv ANTIDOTE_HOME
fpath+=( "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/ohmy/ohmy/plugins/macos/functions" )
builtin autoload -Uz $fpath[-1]/*(N.:t)
fpath+=( "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/ohmy/ohmy/plugins/macos" )
source "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/ohmy/ohmy/plugins/macos/macos.plugin.zsh"
%
```

### fpath-rule:append/prepend

```zsh
% # append
% antidote-script --fpath-rule append --path plugins/docker ohmy/ohmy | subenv ANTIDOTE_HOME
fpath+=( "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/ohmy/ohmy/plugins/docker" )
source "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/ohmy/ohmy/plugins/docker/docker.plugin.zsh"
% # prepend
% antidote-script --fpath-rule prepend --path plugins/docker ohmy/ohmy | subenv ANTIDOTE_HOME
fpath=( "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/ohmy/ohmy/plugins/docker" $fpath )
source "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/ohmy/ohmy/plugins/docker/docker.plugin.zsh"
% # whoops
% antidote-script --fpath-rule foobar --path plugins/docker ohmy/ohmy 2>&1
antidote: error: unexpected fpath rule: 'foobar'
%
```

### pre/post functions

```zsh
% # pre
% antidote-script --pre run_before foo/bar | subenv ANTIDOTE_HOME
run_before
fpath+=( "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/foo/bar" )
source "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/foo/bar/bar.plugin.zsh"
% # post
% antidote-script --post run_after foo/bar | subenv ANTIDOTE_HOME
fpath+=( "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/foo/bar" )
source "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/foo/bar/bar.plugin.zsh"
run_after
%
```

If a plugin is deferred, so is its post event

pre-event
if ! (( $+functions[zsh-defer] )); then

fi
fpath+=( "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/foo/bar" )
zsh-defer source "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/foo/bar/bar.plugin.zsh"
zsh-defer post-event
%
```

Now I understand the full picture. Let me formulate my plan:

**Problem:** A git wrapper on PATH writes progress to stdout even for `git --quiet`. The key location where this causes issues is `functions/antidote-script:90`:
```zsh
git clone --quiet --recurse-submodules --shallow-submodules $o_branch $giturl $bundle_path
```

This is executed in a context where stdout IS the generated plugin script. If a git wrapper ignores `--quiet` and writes progress messages to stdout, those messages leak into the generated plugin script.

The other git invocations that use command substitution (`$(git ...)`) could also be affected:
- `antidote-update:65` - `url=$(git -C "$bundledir" config remote.origin.url)` 
- `antidote-update:73` - `oldsha=$(git -C "$1" rev-parse --short HEAD)`
- `antidote-update:90` - `newsha=$(git -C "$1" rev-parse --short HEAD)`
- `antidote-list:51` - `url=$(git -C "$bundledir" config remote.origin.url)`
- `__antidote_version:8` - `gitsha=$(git -C "${0:A:h:h}" rev-parse --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)`

But the problem description specifically says "leaking that text into generated plugin scripts". This points to `antidote-script:90` as the primary target.

However, let me think about what the right fix is:
- For `antidote-script`: The git clone output should NOT go to stdout. We should redirect git clone stdout to /dev/null. We already print a cloning message to stderr on line 89.
- For command substitutions: If a wrapper writes progress to stdout, it could break things. But the fix here is trickier because we need the actual git output.

Wait, let me reconsider. The issue is "A git wrapper on PATH writes progress to stdout even for `git --quiet`". This means:
- For `git clone --quiet`, the wrapper outputs progress to stdout despite `--quiet`
- For `git rev-parse --short HEAD`, the wrapper may also pollute stdout
- For `git config`, the wrapper may also pollute stdout

The fix should:
1. For `antidote-script:90`: Redirect stdout of git clone to /dev/null (or stderr). This is the critical fix because stdout is the generated script.
2. For command substitutions in other functions: These are less critical for "leaking into generated plugin scripts" but could break functionality. However, the problem statement focuses on script generation.

Let me focus on `antidote-script:90` as the main fix. Additionally, I should check if there are other places where git output leaks into generated scripts.

Looking at `antidote-bundle` (lines 42-48):

`__antidote_parse_bundles` generates `antidote-script` invocations. So the actual git cloning happens within `antidote-script` when it's called.

Let me also check if the clone happens inside command substitution anywhere that feeds the generated script. Looking at `__antidote_parse_bundles`:

```

So `antidote-script` is called and its stdout (including any leaked git output) goes into the generated script. This confirms the fix should be in `antidote-script`.

**Fix for `antidote-script:90`:**
Change:
```zsh
git clone --quiet --recurse-submodules --shallow-submodules $o_branch $giturl $bundle_path
```
To:

#!/bin/zsh
#function t_setup {
  emulate -L zsh
  setopt local_options extended_glob glob_dots

  0=${(%):-%x}
  typeset -g T_PRJDIR="${0:A:h:h:h}"
  typeset -g T_TESTDATA=$T_PRJDIR/tests/testdata
  local testdir="$T_PRJDIR/tests"

  # save path/fpath
  typeset -ga T_PREV_PATH=( $path )
  typeset -ga T_PREV_FPATH=( $fpath )

  # save zstyles, and clear them all for the test session
  typeset -ga T_PREV_ZSTYLES=( ${(@f)"$(zstyle -L ':antidote:*')"} )
  source <(zstyle -L ':antidote:*' | awk '{print "zstyle -d",$2}')

  # setup test functions
  fpath+=( $testdir/functions )
  autoload -Uz $testdir/functions/*

  # mock git
  function git { mockgit "$@" }

  # works with BSD and GNU gmktemp
  T_TEMPDIR=${$(mktemp -d -t t_antidote.XXXXXXXX):A}
  typeset -g T_PREV_HOME=$HOME
  typeset -g T_PREV_ZDOTDIR=$ZDOTDIR

  export HOME=$T_TEMPDIR
  export ZDOTDIR=$HOME/.zsh
  typeset -g ANTIDOTE_HOME=$HOME/.cache/antidote

  # put tmp_home into position
  for p in $testdir/tmp_home/*; do
    cp -rf $p $T_TEMPDIR
  done

  # put testdata into position
  cp -rf -- $T_PRJDIR/tests $T_TEMPDIR/tests

  # rename .mockgit to .git
  local mockdir
  for mockdir in $T_TEMPDIR/**/.mock*; do
    mv $mockdir ${mockdir:s/.mock/.}
  done

  # our mock plugins use this
  typeset -ga plugins=()
  typeset -ga libs=()

  # setup antidote
  zstyle ':antidote:tests' set-warn-options 'on'
  zstyle ':antidote:tests' cloning 'off'
  zstyle ':antidote:bundle' use-friendly-names on
  zstyle ':antidote:defer' bundle 'getantidote/zsh-defer'

  # start from tmp home
  pushd
  cd $T_TEMPDIR

  # source antidote
  source $T_PRJDIR/antidote.zsh
#}

#!/bin/zsh

### Clone bundle(s) and generate the static load script.
#
# usage: antidote bundle [-h|--help] <bundle>...
#

### Clone bundle(s) and generate the static load script.
#function antidote-bundle {
  # Download a bundle and prints its Zsh source line.
  emulate -L zsh; setopt local_options $_adote_funcopts

  local o_help
  zparseopts $_adote_zparopt_flags -- h=o_help -help=h || return 1

  if (( $#o_help )); then
    antidote-help bundle
    return
  fi

  # handle bundles as newline delimited arg strings,
  # or as <redirected or piped| input
  local -a bundles=("${(@f)$(__antidote_collect_input "$@")}")
  (( $#bundles )) || return 1

  # output static file compilation
  local -a zcompile_script=(
    "function {"
    '  0=${(%):-%x}'
    '  local staticfile=${0:A}'
    '  [[ -e ${staticfile} ]] || return 1'
    '  if [[ ! -s ${staticfile}.zwc || ${staticfile} -nt ${staticfile}.zwc ]]; then'
    '    builtin autoload -Uz zrecompile'
    '    zrecompile -pq ${staticfile}'
    '  fi'
    '}'
  )
  if zstyle -t ':antidote:static' zcompile; then
    printf '%s\n' $zcompile_script
  fi

  # antidote-script also clones, but this way we can do it all at once in parallel!
  if (( $#bundles > 1 )); then
    source <(printf '%s\n' $bundles | __antidote_bulk_clone)
  fi

  # generate bundle script
  source <(printf '%s\n' $bundles | __antidote_parse_bundles)
#}

display.text:
#!/bin/zsh

### Parse antidote's bundle DSL.
#function __antidote_parse_bundles {
  emulate -L zsh; setopt local_options $_adote_funcopts

  # Declare vars
  local bundle_str bundle_repr collected_input err lineno=0 skip_load_defer=0
  local key val
  local -a bundles
  local -A bundle

  # Get piped/passed bundles
  collected_input="$(__antidote_collect_input "$@")"
  if [[ -n "$collected_input" ]]; then
    bundles=( "${(@f)collected_input}" )
  else
    bundles=()
  fi
  if ! (( $#bundles )) ; then
    print -ru2 -- "antidote: error: bundle argument expected"
    return 1
  fi

  # Loop through bundles
  for bundle_str in $bundles; do
    (( lineno += 1 ))

    # Parse the bundle.
    bundle_repr=$(__antidote_parser "$bundle_str"); err=$?
    if [[ -z "$bundle_repr" ]]; then
      continue
    elif [[ "$err" -ne 0 ]]; then
      print -ru2 -- "antidote: Bundle parser error on line ${lineno}: '$bundle_str'"
      return 1
    fi

    # Turn the typeset repr into the bundle assoc_arr
    eval "$bundle_repr"

    # move flags to front and call antidote-script
    print -rn -- "antidote-script"
    for key in ${(ok)bundle}; do
      [[ "$key" != name ]] && [[ "$key" != '_'* ]] || continue
      val="${bundle[$key]}"
      if [[ "$val" == "${(q)val}" ]]; then
        printf ' --%s %s' $key $val
      else
        printf ' --%s %s' $key ${(qqq)val}
      fi
    done

    # Add flag for first defer
    if [[ "${bundle[kind]}" == "defer" ]]; then
      if [[ "$skip_load_defer" -eq 0 ]]; then
        skip_load_defer=1
      else
        printf ' --skip-load-defer'
      fi
    fi

    # Escape leading '$' variables
    if [[ "${bundle[name]}" == '$'* ]]; then
      printf ' \$%s\n' "${bundle[name]#\$}"
    else
      printf ' %s\n' "${bundle[name]}"
    fi
  done
#}

Many 'bundle' tests could just as well just be 'script' tests, so we rely on
'test_script.md' to find scripting issues and use this to test actual bundling,
or things not handled by 'antidote script'. You can think of 'antidote script' as
handling a single bundle, and 'antidote bundle' handling them in bulk.

### General

```zsh

```zsh

% cat $ZDOTDIR/.zsh_plugins.zsh | subenv  #=> --file testdata/.zsh_plugins.zsh
%

Test \|piping, \<redirection, and --args

```zsh
% ANTIDOTE_HOME=$HOME/.cache/antidote

% zstyle ':antidote:bundle' use-friendly-names off

part.text:
Now I have a thorough understanding. Let me formulate the fix and tests.

## The Fix

The primary issue is in `antidote-script:90`. The `git clone --quiet` command runs during script generation and its stdout IS the generated plugin script. If a git wrapper ignores `--quiet` and writes progress to stdout, those messages leak into the generated script.

The fix is to redirect git's stdout to /dev/null:
```zsh
git clone --quiet --recurse-submodules --shallow-submodules $o_branch $giturl $bundle_path >/dev/null
```

This is safe because:
1. We already print a cloning progress message to stderr on line 89: `print -ru2 "# antidote cloning $bundle_name..."`
2. We check the exit code on line 91
3. Normal git clone output (like "Cloning into 'foo'...") goes to stderr anyway with `--quiet`, so redirecting stdout only changes behavior for broken wrappers
4. Actual error messages from git go to stderr, which remains visible

Also, I should also consider the command substitution cases:
- `antidote-update:65`: `url=$(git -C "$bundledir" config remote.origin.url)` 
- `antidote-update:73`: `oldsha=$(git -C "$1" rev-parse --short HEAD)`
- `antidote-update:90`: `newsha=$(git -C "$1" rev-parse --short HEAD)`
- `antidote-list:51`: `url=$(git -C "$bundledir" config remote.origin.url)`
- `__antidote_version:8`: `gitsha=$(git -C "${0:A:h:h}" rev-parse --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)`

These use command substitution `$()`, so any stdout leakage would corrupt the captured value. However, the issue description says "leaking that text into generated plugin scripts", which is about `antidote-script`. The command substitution issues would cause different bugs (wrong version strings, broken URLs) rather than "leaking into generated plugin scripts."

Let me focus on the critical fix and see if the user wants me to also fix the command substitution cases.

Actually, the problem statement says "Fix it without hiding useful diagnostics." So the fix should not redirect stderr (which has useful diagnostics), only stdout (which shouldn't have progress output). For `git clone --quiet`, the useful progress is on stderr, and stdout should be empty. Redirecting stdout to /dev/null is the right approach.

Now, for tests. Let me add a regression test that verifies git stdout doesn't leak into generated scripts.

I'll add a test to `test_cmd_script.md` that:
1. Overrides the git function with a noisy mock that writes to stdout even with --quiet
2. Calls `antidote-script` for a new (non-existent) bundle to trigger cloning
3. Verifies the output doesn't contain the noise

Let me also look at what happens when we call `antidote-script` for a new repo. When `bar/foo` is passed to `antidote-script`, the mockgit tries to clone it but has no fake repo for it. So it creates the directory (line 40-41 in mockgit) if `--quiet` is set, or prints an error if not quiet.

Wait, let me re-read the mockgit:

```zsh
elif [[ "$1" = "clone" ]]; then
    local giturl="$2"
    local repo="${giturl:h:t}/${${giturl:t}%.git}"
    local bundledir="${3:-.}"
    local gitsite_repodir="$prjdir/tests/fakegitsite.com/${repo}"
    if [[ -d "$gitsite_repodir" ]]; then
      [[ -d "${bundledir:A}" ]] || mkdir -p "${bundledir:A}"
      cp -r -- "$gitsite_repodir" "${bundledir:A}"
    elif ! (( $#o_quiet )); then
      echo "MOCKGIT: Cloning into '${repo:t}'..."
      echo "MOCKGIT: Repository not found."
      echo "MOCKGIT: repository '$giturl' not found"
    fi
```

So for a repo that doesn't exist in fakesite:
- If `--quiet` is set: silent, does nothing (no output, doesn't even create directory or copy)
- If `--quiet` NOT set: prints mock messages to stdout

For the regression test, I need to simulate a git wrapper that prints to stdout despite `--quiet`. I'll create an enhanced mock that does this and use it instead of the default mock.

But also, I need to think about what `antidote-script` does when cloning a repo that doesn't exist in the fake site. Looking at `antidote-script`:

```zsh
if [[ "$bundle_type" == (repo|url|sshurl) ]] && [[ ! -e "$bundle_path" ]]; then
    local giturl=$(__antidote_tourl $bundle)
    print -ru2 "# antidote cloning $bundle_name..."
    git clone --quiet --recurse-submodules --shallow-submodules $o_branch $giturl $bundle_path
    [[ $? -eq 0 ]] || return 1
fi
```

For `foo/bar`:
- `__antidote_bundle_type foo/bar` returns `repo`
- `__antidote_tourl foo/bar` returns `https://git-config.com/foo/bar` (based on `gitsite` zstyle, which defaults to `https://github.com`)
- Hmm, wait, the `gitsite` default is `https://github.com/`. But in the mock, what `gitsite` is used?

Looking at `__antidote_tourl`:
```
local gitsite
zstyle -s ':antidote:repo' gitsite gitsite || gitsite=${ANTIDOTE_GITSITE:-https://github.com/}
```

During tests, the default would be `https://github.com/`. So `foo/bar` becomes `https://github.com/foo/bar`.

Then `__antidote_bundle_dir` returns the local path based on the URL. Let me check that.

Actually, in the test `test_cmd_script.md`, when `antidote-script --kind clone bar/foo` is run:
```
% antidote-script --kind clone bar/foo
# antidote cloning bar/foo...
%
```

The output shows just the cloning message on stderr, and no stdout. Since the mockgit with `--quiet` is silent for unknown repos, the clone "succeeds" (exit 0, no output), then `antidote-script` skips to the end and returns.

But wait - does the mockgit for `clone` create a directory? If the repo is NOT found in the fake site and `--quiet` is set, then the elif branch is skipped (because `! (( $#o_quiet ))` is false), so nothing happens. But the directory would not be created. Then the `[[ $? -eq 0 ]]` check passes (exit 0). Then the code fall through to:
```
if [[ "$o_kind[-1]" == "clone" ]]; then
    ...
    return
fi
```

So for kind:clone, it returns after cloning (or attempt to clone). For kind:zsh (default), it proceeds to generate the script. But since the clone didn't actually create anything, `bundle_path` wouldn't exist... Actually wait, `__antidote_bundle_dir` generates a path, and `antidote-script` checks `[[ ! -e "$bundle_path" ]]` before cloning. If the mockgit silently does nothing for an unknown repo, the bundle_path still doesn't exist, which could cause issues later. But for `--kind clone`, the code returns immediately.

For the regression test, I need to verify that even when a git wrapper writes noise to stdout, the generated script output is clean. Let me create a test that:
1. Overrides the git function with one that always writes "NOISY GIT OUTPUT" to stdout during clone (ignoring --quiet)
2. Runs `antidote-script` for a known fake repo (like `fakeuser/fakerepo`)
3. Verifies the output is clean (no "NOISY GIT OUTPUT" in the script)

Let me think about which fake repo to use. `fakeuser/fakerepo` exists in `tests/fakegitsite.com/fakeuser/fakerepo`. Let me check what's in it.

Actually wait, let me check how the mockgit maps the giturl. The mockgit creates a repo path from the URL:
```zsh
local repo="${giturl:h:t}/${${giturl:t}%.git}"
local gitsite_repodir="$prjdir/tests/fakegitsite.com/${repo}"
```

For `https://github.com/fakeuser/fakerepo`:
- `giturl:h:t` = `github.com` (hmm, that's wrong)
- Actually let me trace:
  - `giturl = "https://github.com/fakeuser/fakerepo"`
  - `${giturl:h}` = `"https://github.com/fakeuser"` (remove the last path component)
  - `${giturl:h:t}` = `"fakeuser"` (just the last component)  
  - `${giturl:t}` = `"fakerepo"`
  - `${${giturl:t}%.git}` = `"fakerepo"` (remove .git suffix if present)
  - So `repo = "fakeuser/fakerepo"`
  - `gitsite_repodir = "$prjdir/tests/fakegitsite.com/fakeuser/fakerepo"`

And `tests/fakegitsite.com/fakeuser/fakerepo` should exist. Let me verify.

But wait - in the test environment, `__antidote_tourl` uses the `gitsite` zstyle. The default is `https://github.com/`. So `fakeuser/fakerepo` becomes `https://github.com/fakeuser/fakerepo`. The mockgit maps this to `tests/fakegitsite.com/fakeuser/fakerepo`.

Let me check what's in the fake repo.

#!/bin/zsh

### Get the name of the bundle dir.
#function __antidote_bundle_dir {
  emulate -L zsh; setopt local_options $_adote_funcopts

  # If the bundle is a repo/URL, then by default we use the legacy antibody format:
  # `$ANTIDOTE_HOME/https-COLON--SLASH--SLASH-github.com-SLASH-zsh-users-SLASH-zsh-autosuggestions`
  # With `zstyle ':antidote:bundle' use-friendly-names on`, we can simplify to
  # `$ANTIDOTE_HOME/zsh-users/zsh-autosuggestions`
  # If the bundle is a file, use its parent directory.
  # Otherwise, just assume the bundle is a directory.
  local MATCH MBEGIN MEND; local -a match mbegin mend  # appease 'warn_create_global'

  local bundle="$1"
  local bundle_type="$(__antidote_bundle_type $bundle)"

#function __antidote_bundle_dir {

  # With `zstyle ':antidote:bundle' use-friendly-names on`, we can simplify to

  # If the bundle is a file, use its parent directory.

  local MATCH MBEGIN MEND; local -a match mbegin mend  # appease 'warn_create_global'

  local bundle_type="$(__antidote_bundle_type $bundle)"

  # handle repo bundle paths
  if [[ "$bundle_type" == (repo|url|sshurl) ]] && [[ ! -e "$bundle_path" ]]; then
    if zstyle -t ':antidote:bundle' use-friendly-names; then
      # user/repo format
      # ex: $ANTIDOTE_HOME/zsh-users/zsh-autosuggestions
      bundle=${bundle%.git}
      bundle=${bundle:gs/\:/\/}
      local parts=( ${(ps./.)bundle} )
      if [[ $#parts -gt 1 ]]; then
        print $(antidote-home)/${parts[-2]}/${parts[-1]}
      else

    else

      # ex: $ANTIDOTE_HOME/https-COLON--SLASH--SLASH-github.com-SLASH-zsh-users-SLASH-zsh-autosuggestions
      local url=$(__antidote_tourl $bundle)
      url=${url%.git}
      url=${url:gs/\@/-AT-}
      url=${url:gs/\:/-COLON-}
      url=${url:gs/\//-SLASH-}
      print $(antidote-home)/$url
    fi
  elif [[ -f "$bundle" ]]; then

#!/bin/zsh

### Determine bundle type:
### - ?       - unknown
### - empty   - empty string
### - file    - an existing file
### - dir     - an existing directory
### - path    - an non-existant path
### - relpath - a relative path
### - repo    - a git repo (user/repo format)
### - sshurl  - a git repo (SSH format)
### - url     - a git repo (URL format)
### - word    - a word
#function __antidote_bundle_type {
  emulate -L zsh; setopt local_options $_adote_funcopts
  local bundle=$1

  # Try to expand path bundles with '$' and '~' prefixes so that we get a more
  # granular result than 'path'.
  if [[ $bundle == '~/'* ]]; then
    bundle="${HOME}/${bundle#\~/*}"
  elif [[ $bundle == '$'* ]] && [[ $bundle != *'('* ]] && [[ $bundle != *';'* ]]; then
    bundle=$(eval print $bundle)
  fi

  # Determine the bundle type.
  local result
  if [[ -e "$bundle" ]]; then
    [[ -f $bundle ]] && result=file || result=dir
  elif [[ -z "${bundle// }" ]]; then
    result=empty
  else
    case "$bundle" in
      (/|~|'$')*)  result=path     ;;
      *://*)       result=url      ;;
      *@*:*/*)     result=sshurl   ;;
      *(:|@)*)     result='?'      ;;
      */*/*)       result=relpath  ;;
      */)          result=relpath  ;;
      */*)         result=repo     ;;
      *)           result=word     ;;
    esac
  fi

  typeset -g REPLY=$result
  print $result
#}

0=${(%):-%x}
setopt extended_glob

zparseopts -D -M -- -unit=o_unit -rev=o_rev || return 1

display.text:
#!/usr/bin/env zsh
0=${(%):-%x}
setopt extended_glob

local T_PRJDIR="${0:A:h:h}"
cd "$T_PRJDIR"

local o_unit o_rev
zparseopts -D -M -- -unit=o_unit -rev=o_rev || return 1

testfiles=()
if (( $# > 0 )); then
  testfiles=($@)
elif (( $#o_unit )); then
  testfiles=($T_PRJDIR/tests/README.md $T_PRJDIR/tests/test_*.md~*test_real*~*foo*)
else
  testfiles=($T_PRJDIR/tests/README.md $T_PRJDIR/tests/test_*.md)
fi

# if tests are run in reverse order, I can catch places where I didn't teardown properly
if (( $#o_rev )); then
  testfiles=(${(O)testfiles})
fi

# foo example test command
# env -i PATH=$PATH FPATH=$FPATH \
#  zsh -f -- =clitest --list-run --progress dot --prompt '%' --color always $T_PRJDIR/tests/foo.md

# Use ZSH_BINARY if set, otherwise fallback to default zsh
ZSH=${ZSH_BINARY:-zsh}

env -i PATH=$PATH FPATH=$FPATH PAGER=cat \
    $ZSH -f -- \
    =clitest \
        --list-run --progress dot --prompt '%' \
        --color always \
        --pre-flight 'git --version; print $T_PRJDIR $VENDOR $OSTYPE =$ZSH $ZSH_VERSION $ZSH_PATCHLEVEL' \
        -- $testfiles

input.oldString:
## Git wrapper that ignores --quiet

Ensure a git wrapper that writes progress to stdout (ignoring --quiet)
does not leak noise into generated plugin scripts.

Override git with a noisy wrapper:

```zsh
% function git {
  local -a args=("$@")
  if [[ "$args[1]" == "clone" ]]; then
    echo "STDOUT NOISE FROM GIT WRAPPER"
    mockgit "$@" 2>/dev/null
  else
    mockgit "$@"
  fi
}
%
```

Clone only (--kind clone) must not leak stdout noise:

```zsh
% antidote-script --kind clone testy/mctestface 2>/dev/null
%
```

Full script generation must not leak stdout noise:

```zsh
% antidote-script --kind zsh fakeuser/fakerepo 2>/dev/null | subenv ANTIDOTE_HOME
fpath+=( "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/fakeuser/fakerepo" )
source "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/fakeuser/fakerepo/fakerepo.plugin.zsh"
%
```

+## Git wrapper that ignores --quiet
+
+Ensure a git wrapper that writes progress to stdout (ignoring --quiet)
+does not leak noise into generated plugin scripts.
+
+Override git with a noisy wrapper:
+
+```zsh
+% function git { local -a args=("$@"); if [[ "$args[1]" == "clone" ]]; then echo "STDOUT NOISE FROM GIT WRAPPER"; mockgit "$@" 2>/dev/null; else mockgit "$@"; fi }
+%
+```
+
+Clone only (--kind clone) must not leak stdout noise:
+
+```zsh
+% antidote-script --kind clone testy/mctestface 2>/dev/null
+%
+```
+
+Full script generation must not leak stdout noise:
+
+```zsh
+% antidote-script --kind zsh fakeuser/fakerepo 2>/dev/null | subenv ANTIDOTE_HOME
+fpath+=( "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/fakeuser/fakerepo" )
+source "$ANTIDOTE_HOME/fakeuser/fakerepo/fakerepo.plugin.zsh"
+%
+```
+

git add functions/antidote-script tests/test_cmd_script.md && git commit -m "Fix git clone stdout leaking into generated plugin scripts

stderr and antidote already prints its own diagnostic message to stderr."

part.text: Done. Let me summarize what was done.

Done. Here's a summary:

**Root cause:** `functions/antidote-script:90` — `git clone --quiet` runs during script generation where stdout **is** the generated plugin script. A git wrapper that ignores `--quiet` and writes progress to stdout would leak that text directly into the output.

**Fix:** Added `>/dev/null` to the `git clone` command. Git errors go to stderr (preserved), and antidote already prints its own diagnostic `# antidote cloning ...` to stderr on line 89.

**Test coverage:** Added a regression test in `tests/test_cmd_script.md` that overrides git with a noisy wrapper (prints `STDOUT NOISE FROM GIT WRAPPER` to stdout on every clone, ignoring `--quiet`), then verifies both `--kind clone` and full `--kind zsh` paths produce clean output.

**Commit:** `ebc0f0e` — all 684 tests pass.
